codebase_summary: The repository is documentation-only. It defines an authenticated OpenTelemetry gateway product for agent telemetry, including auth-api, OTLP ingress, Collector gateway, SigNoz backend, client onboarding generators, dashboards, operations, retention, and troubleshooting contracts.

components.name: auth-api
components.summary: FastAPI and SQLite control-plane service for users, opaque tokens, validation, trusted identity headers, and ingest audit rows.

components.name: authenticated-ingress
components.summary: Nginx or Caddy OTLP/HTTP ingress that authenticates through auth-api, preserves payloads, and overwrites trusted telemetry headers.

components.name: collector-gateway
components.summary: OpenTelemetry Collector contrib gateway with metadata enrichment, resource attributes, agent normalization, batching, retry, and SigNoz export.

components.name: signoz-backend
components.summary: SigNoz self-hosted backend for logs, traces, metrics, dashboards, retention, and ClickHouse-backed storage.

components.name: client-onboarding
components.summary: Codex and Claude Code config templates and installers with content-minimal defaults and explicit max-capture profile.

components.name: operations-and-validation
components.summary: Make targets, smoke tests, troubleshooting, health checks, backup notes, retention guardrails, and team trial acceptance checks.

features.name: Token control plane
features.summary: Manage users and opaque per-user telemetry tokens with revocation, expiry, scopes, capture profile, and audit rows.
features.intended_behavior: Operators can add users, issue tokens, list/revoke tokens, disable users, and validate bearer tokens without exposing a public admin UI.
features.user_or_system_value: Provides per-user attribution and revocation for all incoming agent telemetry.
known_risks: Token secrets must not be stored in plaintext.
Revoked, expired, disabled-user, and scoped token failures must be deterministic.
Max-capture profile must be explicit and short-lived.

features.name: Authenticated OTLP ingress
features.summary: Accept only standard OTLP/HTTP signal paths, authenticate via subrequest, and forward trusted identity metadata to the Collector.
features.intended_behavior: Invalid tokens fail, valid tokens preserve OTLP payloads, spoofed identity headers are overwritten, and source IP is derived from a trusted chain.
features.user_or_system_value: Prevents unauthenticated ingestion and ensures attribution data is trusted.
tests: infra/nginx tests or container config checks
known_risks: Direct Collector or SigNoz ingestion exposure bypasses auth.
Client-supplied X-Forwarded-For can spoof source IP if not reconstructed.

features.name: Collector gateway enrichment
features.summary: Receive OTLP/HTTP from ingress, copy trusted request metadata into resource attributes, normalize agent.tool, batch, queue, and export to SigNoz.
features.intended_behavior: Every signal exported to SigNoz has trusted user, team, token, source IP, capture profile, and normalized agent tool attributes.
features.user_or_system_value: Makes telemetry queryable by user, team, token, and tool while keeping native agent fields.
entry_points: Collector OTLP/HTTP receiver on internal port 4318
known_risks: Resource processor context keys must match forwarded header names.
Exporter queue and retry health must be observable.

features.name: SigNoz backend bootstrap
features.summary: Provide local SigNoz startup instructions or Compose profile while keeping SigNoz ingestion internal.
features.intended_behavior: Operators can start SigNoz locally and view telemetry received through the authenticated gateway.
features.user_or_system_value: Gives the team a single OpenTelemetry-native UI and storage backend for the trial.
known_risks: Publishing SigNoz OTLP ports would bypass auth.
Retention defaults must be deliberate for trial volume.

features.name: Client onboarding generators
features.summary: Generate Codex and Claude Code telemetry configuration with content-minimal defaults and explicit max-capture overlays.
features.intended_behavior: A token issuance flow prints client snippets; installers back up existing config and apply only managed telemetry settings.
features.user_or_system_value: Reduces onboarding friction and prevents unsafe default content capture.
known_risks: Installer must not overwrite unrelated user config.
Prompt, tool content, and raw API body capture must stay opt-in.
doc_code_mismatches: No templates or installers exist yet.

features.name: Dashboards and data model
features.summary: Standardize resource attributes and starter SigNoz dashboards for team, tool, user, Codex, Claude, and Collector health views.
features.intended_behavior: Operators can filter telemetry by team, user, token, tool, capture profile, and inspect ingest/Collector health.
features.user_or_system_value: Makes the trial answer operational questions without custom queries for every investigation.
known_risks: High-cardinality fields must not become default metric group-bys.
Dashboard JSON schema must be validated against SigNoz.

features.name: Operations, retention, and smoke testing
features.summary: Provide local commands, smoke tests, troubleshooting, backup notes, retention guardrails, and security acceptance checks.
features.intended_behavior: Operators can start, stop, inspect, smoke test, onboard, revoke tokens, and detect ingestion or backpressure problems.
features.user_or_system_value: Turns the repo into a repeatable team trial rather than a manual configuration bundle.
tests: direct Collector and SigNoz OTLP ports are not published
known_risks: Smoke tests must verify security properties, not only happy-path ingest.
Retention guidance must stay aligned with capture profiles.
doc_code_mismatches: No operational scripts or Makefile exist yet.

features.name: Optional warehouse deferral
features.summary: Document the optional ClickHouse warehouse profile while keeping it outside the v1 implementation path.
features.intended_behavior: The team does not duplicate SigNoz storage unless a concrete downstream SQL requirement appears.
features.user_or_system_value: Prevents premature storage expansion during the trial.
known_risks: Adding this too early doubles storage and operational surface.

unknowns: No implementation package manager is selected beyond the planned FastAPI and Python CLI direction.
No CI provider or job matrix exists.
No SigNoz dashboard JSON has been validated against a live SigNoz instance.
Codex telemetry config must be re-verified against the installed CLI before shipping the installer.
No deployment environment or TLS termination layer is implemented.
No database migration mechanism is selected.

evidence.summary: Defines the product scope, target telemetry endpoint, planned developer path, and planned repository shape.
Defines the request flow, trust boundaries, ports, and baseline Collector config.
Defines opaque token format, database schema, validation flow, headers, CLI contract, and audit requirements.
Defines resource attributes, capture profiles, cardinality guidance, and dashboard dimensions.
Defines Codex and Claude Code config snippets, installer requirements, normal/max capture behavior, and onboarding verification.
Defines deliverables and acceptance criteria for the MVP and follow-on trial operations.
Records that the repo is currently documentation-only and lists planned source boundaries.
